query: HarmonyOS ArkTS/ArkUI platform fact check. API level: 22. Stage goal: verify exact local API usage while implementing. Focus: Reading the effective color mode at page appear in ArkTS when EntryAbility sets COLOR_MODE_NOT_SET (follow system): does UIAbilityContext.config.colorMode return the resolved DARK/LIGHT or the raw NOT_SET(-1) at aboutToAppear, and is getUIContext().getHostContext() a valid UIAbilityContext to read it from?. Task constraint: Initial isDarkMode must reflect the effective system/app color mode at aboutToAppear (deep-color scenario 1). System-follow is set via EntryAbility onCreate setColorMode(COLOR_MODE_NOT_SET). Decision: set isDarkMode = (ctx.config.colorMode === COLOR_MODE_DARK) where ctx = getUIContext().getHostContext() as common.UIAbilityContext.. Project evidence: Index.ets defines COLOR_MODE_DARK=0, COLOR_MODE_LIGHT=1; toggleTheme already does const context = this.getUIContext().getHostContext() as common.UIAbilityContext; context.getApplicationContext().setColorMode(isDarkMode?COLOR_MODE_DARK:COLOR_MODE_LIGHT). EntryAbility.onCreate calls getApplicationContext().setColorMode(ConfigurationConstant.ColorMode.COLOR_MODE_NOT_SET). UI binds via $r() with base/ and dark/ element/color.json palettes.. Platform surfaces: @kit.AbilityKit UIAbilityContext.config (Configuration.colorMode); ConfigurationConstant.ColorMode (NOT_SET=-1, DARK=0, LIGHT=1); ApplicationContext.setColorMode; getUIContext().getHostContext() return type. Extra constraints: Confirm the exact member path config.colorMode and its enum values at runtime when the app follows the system (NOT_SET was set at onCreate).; Confirm getUIContext().getHostContext() returns a UIAbilityContext whose .config.colorMode is readable at aboutToAppear (before any user toggle).; Flag any case where colorMode stays NOT_SET(-1) after setColorMode(NOT_SET) so the equality check isDarkMode = (===DARK) would yield false on a dark system.; Do not redesign; only confirm the exact read path and return/error/null/default semantics.. Check only: exact import/module/type/member/signature and minimal call shape; API-level availability for this call; required permission/config/context; return/error/null/default/conflict semantics; call-site lifecycle/threading constraints; compile/runtime failure traps. Quality bar: a coder can write or check the local code from the answer: exact import/module/type/member/signature, minimal call shape, context/permission/config, lifecycle/threading, return/error/null/default/conflict semantics, forbidden local usage, and validation check. Return concise evidence bullets only. Use these slots when applicable: exact_api, exact_import, return_error, context_permission, lifecycle_threading, local_forbidden, local_validation, planned_fallback. For critical slots, write `slot: none` when the fact is not applicable or not known. If sources disagree or the fact is unavailable, state the conflict or unknown explicitly; do not smooth it over. You may add extra:<name> if a critical fact does not fit. Do not invent missing facts. Max 8 bullets. Format each bullet as '- slot: fact'. No preface, headings, examples, tutorials, or steps.
